description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular form and a prominent convex glass screen. The casing is primarily finished in a warm, wood-like veneer on the top and sides, while the front and rear panels are made of beige plastic. The front features a thick bezel framing the screen, with a set of control buttons and dials located on the lower right. The top panel has a set of rectangular buttons or switches, likely for power and channel selection. The back panel is vented for heat dissipation and includes various ports and connectors, as well as a carrying handle. The overall design is characteristic of mid-20th-century analog televisions, with sturdy construction and utilitarian styling.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a wood-veneer casing, convex glass screen, and beige plastic front and back panels featuring control buttons and venting.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, thick beige bezel, and control buttons below the screen.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the wood-veneer panel and the top-mounted rectangular buttons.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the beige vented back panel, carrying handle, and input/output ports.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the wood-veneer panel and a large vent near the bottom.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick beige plastic bezel with a smooth finish and utilitarian styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of buttons and dials on the lower right front edge and a row of rectangular buttons on the top panel.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
